latest.title: Placing and Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 12:15
RNS Number : 2334A
Sunrise Resources Plc
13 April 2026
13 April 2026
SUNRISE RESOURCES PLC
("Sunrise" or the "Company")
Placing to Raise
£225,000
Director/PDMR Holdings
Issue of Equity and TVR
Sunrise Resources plc (AIM: SRES), wishes to announce that it has conditionally raised £225,000 before expenses ("the Placing") through the issue of 900,000,000 new Ordinary Shares of 0.001p each (the "Placing Shares") at the price of 0.025 pence per Placing Share (the "Placing Price"). The Placing Price is equal to the market closing bid price of the Company's Ordinary Shares on 10 April 2026 (being the latest practicable date prior to this announcement). The Placing was arranged through the Company's broker, AlbR Capital Limited ("AlbR").
The Board values its retail investor base and is also conscious of the need to provide existing shareholders with the opportunity to participate in any capital raise being undertaken by the Company, where practically and commercially feasible. With this in mind, should any existing shareholder wish to subscribe for new Ordinary shares on the same terms as the Placing, they should contact the Company before 2.00pm on Wednesday 15 April 2026 at Tel: 01625 838 884, or by email to:
info@sunriseresourcesplc.com
.
If there is sufficient additional demand, the Company will consider, but is not obligated, to initiate a retail offer of new Ordinary Shares on the same terms as the Placing through a shareholder retail access platform.
The Placing
Mr Patrick Cheetham, Executive Chairman of the Company, and Mr Adam Hainsworth, a non-executive director of the Company, have each subscribed £50,000 for 200,000,000 Placing Shares as part of the Placing at the Placing Price. Mr Cheetham's and Mr Hainsworth's participation in the Placing constitute related party transactions under AIM Rule 13. The independent director of the Company (being Mr James Cole), having consulted with Beaumont Cornish Limited, the Company's nominated adviser, considers that the terms of their participation in the Placing are fair and reasonable insofar as the Company's shareholders are concerned.
Additional Shares
The Company also proposes to issue 468,860,760 new Ordinary Shares at the Placing Price (the "Additional Shares") to settle a number of longer term creditors. This includes 96,256,760 new Ordinary Shares to be issued to past and present directors in settlement of a portion of fees owing for the periods November 2023 to March 2024 and April 2025 to December 2025. The issue of shares to settle Directors fees is proposed pursuant to terms first agreed on 20 August 2008, and in order for the Company to preserve cash for its project activities. It also includes 336,604,000 new Ordinary Shares being issued to Tertiary Minerals plc ("Tertiary") in partial settlement of invoices issued by Tertiary to the Company in respect of management fees. Tertiary provides management services to the Company at cost. Mr Cheetham is also Chairman of Tertiary Minerals plc. Following Admission, Tertiary will hold 358,558,787 Ordinary Shares or 3.91% of the enlarged issued share capital.
The following table shows the number of new Ordinary Shares issued to the current directors and their total beneficial holding in the Company following Admission of the Placing Shares and the Additional Shares.
Director
Number of
Placing and
Additional Shares
Total interest in
Ordinary Shares

issued share
capital following
Admission

Adam Hainsworth
206,457,560
532,195,932
5.80%
Broker Warrants
Under the terms of its engagement dated 1 October 2025, AlbR will be issued with 25,000,000 warrants in connection with the Placing, each warrant entitling the holder to apply for one new Ordinary Share at the Placing Price at any time within 12 months from the date of Admission.
Use of Proceeds
The net funds raised will be applied to general working capital and to support the Company's plans for advancement of the Company's mineral projects.
Admission
The issue of the Placing Shares and the Additional Shares is conditional only on Admission. Application is being made for the Placing Shares and the Additional Shares, which will rank pari passu with all existing ordinary shares, to be admitted to trading on AIM and Admission is expected to occur on or around 17 April 2026.
Total Voting Rights
For the purposes of the Disclosure and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority, the Board of Sunrise hereby notifies the market that further to Admission of the Placing Shares, and the Additional Shares the Company's issued share capital will consist of 9,181,262,596 Ordinary Shares with a nominal value of 0.001p each, with voting rights ("Ordinary Shares"). The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in Treasury. Therefore, the total number of Ordinary Shares in the Company with voting rights will be 9,181,262,596. This figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as denominator for the calculations by which they may determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Further information:
Sunrise Resources plc

Sunrise Resources plc wishes to announce that a Non-Executive Director of the Company, Mr James Cole, has today transferred 72,533,764 ordinary shares in the Company, representing 0.93% of the Company's issued share capital, to a private limited company,

in which he is the sole shareholder. Mr Cole retains 100 ordinary shares registered in his own name.

latest.title: ANNUAL REPORT TO 30 SEPTEMBER 2025 & NOTICE OF AGM
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5406U
Sunrise Resources Plc
27 February 2026
Sunrise Resources Plc
("the Company")
AIM Announcement 27 February 2026
ANNUAL REPORT TO 30 SEPTEMBER 2025
AND NOTICE OF 2026 ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
Sunrise Resources plc announces that the full version of the Annual Report for the year ended 30 September 2025 and the Notice of the 2026 Annual General Meeting ("AGM") has now been published on the Company's website at:
https://www.sunriseresourcesplc.com/shareholder-documents
A letter or email, depending on individual preference, has been sent to registered shareholders to notify them of the publication of the Annual Report and Notice.
The AGM has been convened for 10.00 a.m. on Wednesday 25 March 2026 at Mottram Hall, Wilmslow Road, Mottram, Cheshire SK10 4QT, in the Charlton Room and Hub Lounge.
Proxy Voting will be available from 5.00 p.m. today and instructions can be found starting on page 60 of the Annual Report at the link above.

company_30s.what_they_do: Sunrise Resources is a junior mining explorer that focuses on copper and other base‑metal projects in the United States, mainly the Hazen Project in Nevada and a few smaller royalty interests in the UK. The company’s work is largely geological exploration, drilling, and early‑stage feasibility studies.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Copper is the backbone of modern electrification and renewable infrastructure, so a new source can be highly valuable. Sunrise’s projects sit in a region with strong commodity demand and a history of successful discoveries, giving the company a potentially attractive upside if the geology checks out.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a fundraising phase, having just completed a private equity placing and holding an AGM to decide on further capital needs. Management is dealing in shares, and the company’s latest audit shows continued cash burn with no revenue yet. The stock is trading at the very bottom of its recent range, indicating a severe sell‑off that may have exhausted most short‑term sellers.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy raising money—an equity placing followed by a wrap retail offer—while its AGM was dominated by questions about the directors’ share deals and the future of the Hazen Project.

There are two clear strands: (1) a push to secure fresh capital to fund drilling and feasibility work, and (2) governance pressure from shareholders who feel the directors are diluting ownership without clear upside.

The narrative is shifting from pure survival to a potential turnaround: if the placing closes and the Hazen Project delivers a solid resource estimate, the company could pivot from a cash‑burning explorer to a development candidate.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Investor distrust and a scramble for fresh capital
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No bullish chatter was found; the community is largely skeptical of the current capital‑raising strategy.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears focus on the directors’ share sales, the repeated equity placements, and the lack of any production or proven resources. They argue the company is simply bleeding cash.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood is flat to negative, with a few posts hinting at a potential upside if the equity placing succeeds, but the overall tone remains wary.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Sunrise Resources is flat—no recent search activity, which means the market is not currently focused on this name.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the current equity placing—if the firm raises the targeted £200k, it will have the liquidity to pay for the next drilling phase.
A positive resource estimate from the Hazen drilling program—any announcement of a significant copper discovery would immediately lift the stock.
A clear AGM decision to move the project into a development phase, including a schedule for feasibility studies or a joint‑venture partnership.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 50‑day average, indicating that a new group of investors is stepping in.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume surge that accompanies a price bounce back to the 20‑day moving average. A sharp rise in the share price after the placing closes is a strong confirmation of a mean‑reversion trigger.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early to mid‑stage of a deep crash. The APEX compression score suggests the market has not yet fully absorbed the downside, so a bounce is still plausible once the next catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share is 80‑90% below its recent high, which is a classic depth for a high‑reward bounce. If the equity placing closes and the Hazen Project delivers a solid resource, the price could recover to 50% of the all‑time high, giving roughly a 100% upside. Historically, deep‑bottom signals in the 70‑95% range have produced 2–3× returns on the bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are dilution from the placing, the uncertainty of the Hazen Project’s geology, and the directors’ ongoing share sales. If the project fails to deliver, the stock could stay at the bottom or fall further as new capital is not raised.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the remaining 10‑20% of the current price if the placing fails or the project stalls. Upside could be 2–3× the current level if the project succeeds, giving a risk‑reward ratio of roughly 1:2.5.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the share price breaks above the 20‑day moving average with at least a 30% volume spike, indicating new buyers. Size the position so that a 10% drop below the entry would trigger a stop, protecting the 10‑20% downside. Hold until a clear resource announcement or a second volume spike confirms the recovery.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a very small position until the equity placing is fully subscribed. Watch the AGM minutes for any decision to move into a development phase. If the company announces a resource estimate within the next 30 days, consider a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable—there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. The primary risk is dilution, not a terminal event.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Sunrise Resources sits at a deep sell‑off, and if the upcoming equity placing closes and the Hazen Project delivers a positive resource estimate, the stock could rebound to 50% of its peak, offering a highly asymmetric upside with a small remaining downside.
